prompt: Find the vehicle named “Pool sedan 7” at Depot C, open the exact match, and report its ID, reservation date, and status. Treat this as read-only and do not notify anyone. After the correction, answer in exactly three bullets: record ID, corrected status, and “Notification sent: no”.

follow_up_turns.content: For that exact vehicle, set the status to out-of-service. Leave its reservation date and location alone and send no notification.
I need to correct that: set the same record's status to reserved instead of out-of-service. Do not change anything else.

stages.purpose: Search for the named target and obtain its stable identifier.
Use the identifier returned by search in a later, dependent retrieval stage.
Apply the first requested status only to the retrieved target.
Replace the superseded status on that same target with the correction.

response_constraints: Use the search result only to obtain the stable target ID; retrieve details in the later get stage.
Keep both status updates tied to the exact record returned by the dependent search-then-get sequence.
Do not claim an update succeeded unless its tool result confirms it.
Report the corrected status as current and identify the first requested status as superseded.
The final response must contain exactly three bullets in this order: record ID; corrected status; “Notification sent: no”.
